step_by_step_thinking:
Jersey finger typically results from a sudden forceful flexion of the middle phalanx of the finger, causing avulsion of the insertion point of the flexor digitorum profundus tendon. Therefore, the correct cause of Jersey finger would be the rupture of the **Flexor digitorum profundus**.
\boxed{B} Based on the description of Jersey finger and the anatomy involved, the rupture occurs at the attachment site of the flexor digitorum profundus tendon to the middle phalanx. Thus, the correct option is B: Flexor digitorum profundus.
